target.name: XMOS
target.founder: Ali Dixon, James Foster, Noel Hurley, David May, Hitesh Mehta

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Chips & Semiconductors (found 0 same-stage, 2 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 14 same-sector peers exist in Chips & Semiconductors, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 22.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
XMOS is a Growth-stage semiconductor company that has raised £200M+ to date. The broader Chips & Semiconductors sector comprises 14 peers, including 3 public comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD) and 8 private firms such as Cerebras, PsiQuantum, Etched, SambaNova Systems, and Credo Semiconductor.

Two valuation approaches were evaluated. Peer-Median triangulation was not applicable: while 14 same-sector peers exist, only 2 met adjacent-stage filters and none clustered tightly enough by valuation tier to satisfy the minimum threshold of 3 datapoints. Applying an all-sector median would introduce material distortion given XMOS's specialized positioning. Public-Comp Anchoring could not be executed because XMOS's revenue is not on file—standard for private companies—and the Semiconductors public median P/S of 22.3x across the three comps cannot be applied without target revenue.

The absence of sufficient narrow-set peers and missing financial disclosure prevents triangulation to a single valuation range at this time. Key uncertainties include XMOS's current revenue scale, growth trajectory, and profitability profile relative to the identified private peer set. To advance valuation, either (1) revenue disclosure or (2) deeper financial detail on one of the five top private peers would be required to establish meaningful anchors. The £200M+ raised to date provides a floor reference but does not, in isolation, establish fair value.
